course_description:
Written communication remains a fundamental aspect of work in all professions. Despite the rise of digital alternatives, effective writing is still a vital skill for anyone seeking employment in the 21st century workplace. This course covers key theoretical principles and practical applications of writing in a corporate environment.

This course takes a practical approach to most aspects of writing in the professional workplace. The fundamental importance of correct grammar, punctuation and syntax is taken as a given. The course will cover a broad range of workplace writing genres, including report writing, speechwriting, presentations and correspondence. Topics include creating credibility at word, sentence, paragraph, and document levels; and revising, editing, and proofreading professional documents.

Additional timetable information
Whilst every effort is made to place students in their preferred activity, it is not always possible for a student to be enrolled in their tutorial of choice. If you require assistance, please ensure that you email
timetabling.commarts@enquire.uq.edu.au
from your
UQ student email
with:
Your name
Your student ID
The course code
A list of three tutorial preferences (in order of preference)
Reason for the change – e.g. timetable clash, elite athlete status, SAP
Teaching staff do not have access to change tutorials or help with timetables; all timetabling changes must be processed through the Timetabling Team.
course_aims: This course aims to prepare students for the literacy demands of the service-oriented workplace.

learning_outcomes.description: understand the functional and situational nature of writing in the professional workplace
be familiar with the conventions, styles and formats of the most common professional communications
be able to generate, evaluate, analyse, synthesise, and organise information, using problem-solving strategies
have the skills to create specific professional documents that are accessible to a wide range of readers
understand how professional writing and editing works and be able to take responsibility for your ongoing development as writer/editors in the professions for which you are preparing.

assessment_summary.title: Group discussion & participation
assessment_summary.weight: 20% This assessment is in two parts, each marked Pass/Fail and each worth 10% for a total possible 20% overall.

assessment_summary.title: An annotation (10%) and a short report (20%)

assessment_details.title: Group discussion & participation
assessment_details.weight: 20% This assessment is in two parts, each marked Pass/Fail and each worth 10% for a total possible 20% overall.
assessment_details.task_description: Working in small groups, students will each lead a discussion and set a writing exercise for their tutorial. Presentation times will be allocated by tutors in Week 3, with presentations to take place from Week 5 to Week 13. Full details are in the Assessments folder on Blackboard. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
In-class activity.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Students wishing to change their assigned presentation date must liaise with their tutor before the presentation due date.
Late submission
Late submission is not possible for this piece of assessment. Failure to attend the assigned tutorial and lead the demonstration and discussion will result in a zero mark.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.late_submission: Late submission is not possible for this piece of assessment. Failure to attend the assigned tutorial and lead the demonstration and discussion will result in a zero mark.

assessment_details.title: An annotation (10%) and a short report (20%)
assessment_details.task_description: This assessment is in two parts: Annotation. Students are to annotate a piece of AI-generated text, identifying specific strengths and weaknesses in the text (10%). Short report . (700 words). Students are to write a short report, recommending a course of action with regard to the use of AI-generated text in a work scenario (20%). The AI-generated text, criteria and other important information to complete the assessment are contained in the Assessment 2 folder on Blackboard. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
TurnItIn Receipts:
Assignments for this course will be submitted electronically via Blackboard and using TurnItIn . Before submitting any assignments for this course you must ensure you have completed UQ's compulsory online Academic Integrity Tutorial.
When you successfully submit your assessment to TurnItIn you will see text confirming your submission is complete, before being redirected to your Assignment inbox. On this page you can:
View the name of the submitted file View date and time of the upload Resubmit your paper (if necessary) Download your submitted paper Download digital receipt.
If you cannot see your submission in your Assignment inbox you should regard your submission as unsuccessful. Students are responsible for retaining evidence of submission by the due date for all assessment items, in the required form (e.g. screenshot, email, photo, and an unaltered copy of submitted work).
If the submission was not successful:
Note the error message (preferably take a screenshot). Go to your assignment page and see if it is possible to submit again. If you cannot submit again email your course coordinator immediately with the assignment attached.
Please visit this webpage for further advice on how to submit your TurnItIn assignment .
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Report to a manager
assessment_details.task_description: Write a report based on one of a number of scenarios outlined in the Assessment 3 folder on Blackboard. The assessment is in two parts : A 1500-word report to a manager (40%), and A 250-word explanation for the decisions you have made in drafting and finalising the above report (10%). Briefing documents providing background information to the various scenarios are in the Assessment 3 folder on Blackboard. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.topic: Week 1: Course introduction Writing in the workplace. Introduction and expectations from the course.
Week 1: No tutorial this week
Week 2: The basics Spelling, grammar, punctuation, syntax, style, style guides, editing terminology.
Week 2 Discuss lecture; writing exercises.
Week 3: Language & AI Clarity and words (inclusive, weasel, jargon and otherwise). The place of Artificial Intelligence and generative text in the 21st Century workplace.
Week 3 Discuss lecture; writing exercises.
Week 4: Public holiday Public holiday 14/8. No lecture this week
Week 4: No class Public holiday 14/8. No tutorial this week
Week 5: Report Writing Writing and designing internal and external reports
Week 5 Discuss lecture. Presentations begin. Writing exercises.
Week 6: Editing and proofreading Drafting, editing and proofreading
Week 6 Discuss lecture. Presentations continue. Writing exercises.
Week 7: Speech writing Writing for the ear, as opposed to the eye. Rhetoric and persuasion.
Week 7 Discuss lecture. Presentations continue. Writing exercises.
Week 8: Presentations Writing engaging presentations (and the evils of PowerPoint)
Week 8 Discuss lecture. Presentations continue. Writing exercises.
Week 9: Technical Writing How-to, Style guides and manuals.
Week 9 Discuss lecture. Presentations continue. Writing exercises.
Mid-Semester Break No lectures or tutorials this week
Week 10: Corporate Writing Writing for online; Investor communications
Week 10 Discuss lecture. Presentations continue. Writing exercises.
Week 11: No lecture this week Public holiday 7/10
Week 11: No tutorial this week Public holiday 7/10
Week 12: Internal & External communications Written communications -- both internal and external to the organisation. Professional correspondence: letters, emails, memos
Week 12 Discuss lecture. Presentations continue. Writing exercises.
Week 13: Writing week Writing week: No lecture this week
Week 13 Presentations conclude. Writing workshop. Bring your draft/plan for assessment 3 and discuss approaches to the task with your peers. Get some writing done in a collegiate atmosphere.
